summary: Matched a batch of gm_1B03 game-mode/minor-scene functions by replacing many placeholder stubs with typed implementations, adding a gm_1B03.static.h for local scene data structs, and tightening related gm/mn/un headers. The decomp.dev report says this produced 27 new 100% matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1B03, improved gm_801B1AD4 to 85%, and raised matched code by +2592 bytes (+0.07%).
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_1B03 functions including CSSData helpers gm_801B06B0, gm_801B0730, gm_801B07B4, gm_801B07E8 and minor-scene callbacks such as gm_801B0C74, gm_801B0F60, gm_801B0FB8, gm_801B138C, gm_801B14DC, gm_801B1514, gm_801B154C, gm_801B1588, gm_801B15C8, gm_801B1648, gm_801B1688, gm_801B16C8, gm_801B1724, gm_801B174C, gm_801B1788, gm_801B1810, gm_801B1834, gm_801B18D4, gm_801B1A2C, gm_801B1A84, and gm_801B1B74.
Added src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.static.h with local structs for debug/game-over, debug-results, memcard, sound-test, main-menu-exit, and training-menu data used by gm_1B03 callbacks.
Renamed struct MatchExitInfo2 to ResultsMatchInfo, added its forward declaration, and updated gmresultplayer, gmvsmelee, and gm_1B03 references accordingly.

Replaced raw minor scene IDs in large MinorScene arrays with MN_* enum-style names such as MN_DEBUG_MENU, MN_PRIZE_INTERFACE, MN_VS, MN_RESULTS, MN_INTRO_EASY, MN_INTRO_ALLSTAR, MN_GAMEOVER, MN_APPROACH, MN_MEMCARD, MN_STAFFROLL, MN_MENU, MN_CSS, MN_SSS, MN_SUDDEN_DEATH, MN_TOU, MN_TOU_BRACKET, MN_TOU_FINISH, MN_TRAINING_MODE, and MN_CAMERA_VS.
Recovered more precise prototypes in gm_1B03.h, gm_1601.h, gm_18A5.h, and un_2FC9.h, replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete pointer and integer argument types.
Changed gm_804876D8 and gm_80487810 from raw UNK_T arrays to typed static StartMeleeData and MatchExitInfo objects and updated scene table entries to pass their addresses.

Refined gm/types.h and mn/types.h fields: MatchEnd.x0 became u32 with a timer note, TmData gained explicit x28 stage-id and other fields, CSSData.unk_0x0 gained a tentative 1p-port comment, and SSSData.force_stage_id became s8.
Adjusted gmtou.c indexing from data->pad_x8[...] to the newly exposed TmData fields/padding and changed un_802FF884 to accept a char* argument used by gm_801B0FB8.
smart_moves: Used small, local static-header structs to unblock matching without forcing uncertain debug/menu data layouts into broad public headers.
Converted magic minor-scene numeric constants to MN_* names, improving readability while preserving initializer structure needed for matching.
Recovered types at call boundaries rather than only in gm_1B03.c: ResultsMatchInfo, CSSData, MatchEnd, TmData, SSSData, and function prototypes were updated where callers and callees agreed.
Typed static globals by their actual payload role, then passed addresses in scene tables; this avoided raw array casts and made initializer intent clearer.
Kept uncertain recovered fields documented with tentative comments such as CSSData.unk_0x0 being a possible 1p port, instead of over-naming beyond evidence.
decomp_lessons: For scene-table-heavy gm files, replacing raw scene IDs with existing menu enum names can make the control-flow/data-flow story much clearer and may not disturb matching.
When a placeholder struct is being used specifically for result screen data, a domain name like ResultsMatchInfo is more reusable than a generic MatchExitInfo2.
Matching callbacks often requires recovering the opaque scene payload structs used by gm_801A427C; local static headers are a practical place for partly known payload layouts.
Header prototype cleanup can be necessary for matching: functions that looked like UNK_PARAMS may need exact signedness, pointer types, or return types to match callsites.
Signedness matters in menu structs; this PR changed SSSData.force_stage_id to s8 and several prototypes to s32/s8/u8 where the diff showed concrete usage.
Global storage declarations should match how scene data is addressed. Typed objects plus address-of entries can replace raw UNK_T arrays when the payload type is known.
naming_conventions: Use ResultsMatchInfo for result-screen match data formerly called MatchExitInfo2.
Use MN_* names for minor scene IDs instead of raw numeric constants in MinorScene initializers.
Local/private recovered structs for one translation unit can live in a file-specific .static.h, e.g. gm_1B03.static.h.
Preserve address-based symbol names such as gm_801B06B0 and gm_804876D8 when no semantic function/global name is available.
Keep uncertain member names as x-offset or unk_0x0 with comments when semantics are tentative.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced placeholder stubs marked with /// #gm_... by C implementations for a cluster of small scene callbacks and CSSData accessors.

Used exact scene-payload struct layouts with padding arrays to line up field offsets, including DebugGameOverData, DebugMemcardData, DebugSoundTestData, MainMenuExitData, TrainingMenuData, TmData, SSSData, and ResultsMatchInfo.
Recovered function signatures from usage, including gm_801B06B0/gm_801B0730/gm_801B07B4/gm_801B07E8 CSSData signatures, gm_8018F1B0(MatchEnd*) returning s32, and un_802FF884(char*).
Matched scene table initializers by using typed globals and pointer/address forms, notably StartMeleeData gm_804876D8 and MatchExitInfo gm_80487810.
Used HSD_Randi values and external data such as un_803FA258 in gm_801B0C74 to reproduce game-over/debug data initialization.
The decomp.dev bot reported 27 new 100% gm_1B03 matches and one partial improvement, confirming the tactics were effective.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported matched code at 35.99%, with +0.07% and +2592 bytes from the PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching gm_801B1AD4 after this PR left it at 85%.
Search for further uses or refinements of ResultsMatchInfo after MatchExitInfo2 was renamed.
Look for later PRs that replace remaining gm_1B03 x-offset fields with semantic names.
Check whether CSSData.unk_0x0 was later confirmed as a 1p port or renamed.
Search for follow-up work on TmData fields x28/x32 and gmtou.c indexing.
